---
title: "Modeling Tropical Cyclone Boundary Layer"
subtitle: "A Comprehensive Review"
author: "Liang Hu & Ahsan Kareem"
institute: "NatHaz Modeling Laboratory, University of Notre Dame"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: white
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    width: 1200
    height: 700
    css: slides.css
---

## Overview

- **Tropical cyclones** generate extreme winds threatening civil infrastructure
- **TCBL modeling** is essential for Performance-Based Wind Engineering (PBWE)
- This review bridges **meteorology** and **wind engineering** perspectives

::: {.notes}
This review covers TCBL models from both meteorological and wind engineering perspectives.
:::

---

## Why TCBL Matters

$$G(PI) = \int \int \int \int G(PI \mid DM) f(DM \mid EDP) f(EDP \mid IP, SP, IM) f(IP \mid IM, SP) \, d\lambda(IM)$$

- The **intensity measure** $\lambda(IM)$ drives the entire PBWE framework
- Accurate TCBL modeling → reliable wind hazard assessment
- Monte Carlo simulation requires efficient TCBL models

---

## Multi-scale TC Simulation

| Scale | Spatial | Temporal | Method |
|-------|---------|----------|--------|
| Climate | Global | Months–Years | Statistical genesis |
| Macro | ~1000 km | Days | Track models |
| Meso | ~100 km | Hours | TCBL models |
| Micro | ~1 km | Minutes | LES/ABL models |

---

## TCBL Model Hierarchy

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    A[TCBL Models] --> B[Full-physics]
    A --> C[Diagnostic]
    A --> D[Data-driven]
    B --> B1[WRF-ARW]
    B --> B2[HWRF]
    B --> B3[CM1]
    C --> C1[Parametric]
    C --> C2[Simulation-based]
    C1 --> C1a[Empirical]
    C1 --> C1b[Analytical]
    C2 --> C2a[2D-slab]
    C2 --> C2b[2D Hybrid]
    C2 --> C2c[3D Nonlinear KW01]
    D --> D1[Profile modification]
    D --> D2[POD-based]
```

---

## Full-physics Models

- **WRF-ARW**: Most widely used NWP system for TC simulation
  - Turbulence parameterizations (YSU, MYJ, etc.)
  - Data assimilation critical for accuracy
- **HWRF**: Operational hurricane forecasting
- **HWCM**: Hybrid approach (WRF + vortex following)
- **Limitation**: Computationally expensive for Monte Carlo PBWE

---

## Diagnostic Models — Governing Equations

$$\frac{\partial u}{\partial t} + u\,\frac{\partial u}{\partial r} + \frac{v + v_{gr}}{r}\frac{\partial u}{\partial \varphi} + w\,\frac{\partial u}{\partial z} - \frac{v^2}{r} = \left(f + \frac{2v_{gr}}{r}\right)v + \frac{\partial}{\partial z}\left(K\,\frac{\partial u}{\partial z}\right)$$

$$\frac{\partial v}{\partial t} + u\,\frac{\partial v}{\partial r} + \frac{v + v_{gr}}{r}\frac{\partial v}{\partial \varphi} + w\,\frac{\partial v}{\partial z} + \frac{uv}{r} = -\left(f + \frac{v_{gr}}{r} + \frac{\partial v_{gr}}{\\partial r}\right)u + \frac{\partial}{\partial z}\left(K\,\frac{\partial v}{\partial z}\right)$$

---

## Gradient Wind Profiles

**Holland (1980):**

$$p = p_c + \Delta p \exp\left(-A / r^B\right), \quad RMW = A^{1/B}$$

$$v_{\max} = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho e} \Delta p}$$

**Willoughby (2006):** Piecewise profile with smooth transition

---

## The KW01 3D Nonlinear Model

- **Most theoretically rigorous** TCBL model
- Solves full nonlinear primitive equations
- Captures:
  - Low-level jet (supergradient winds)
  - Asymmetric structure from translation
  - Vertical advection effects
- **Benchmark** for PBWE applications

---

## Data-driven Approaches

- **Profile modification**: Adjusting gradient profiles with observational data
- **Asymmetry introduction**: Adding observed asymmetric patterns
- **POD-based**: Proper Orthogonal Decomposition for wind field reconstruction

$$\text{TARGET}(t) = \text{MFLD} + \sum_{i=1}^{n_s} W_i(t) \text{PC}_i$$

---

## NatHaz Systematic Investigation

Key contributions from NatHaz Modeling Laboratory:

1. Comprehensive validation of KW01 model
2. Turbulence closure optimization
3. Linearization for computational efficiency
4. Surface wind reduction factor analysis
5. GPU acceleration for Monte Carlo simulation

---

## Future Directions

- Integration of full-physics and diagnostic models
- Machine learning for TCBL prediction
- Improved turbulence parameterizations
- Climate change impacts on TC wind hazards
- Real-time PBWE with operational forecasting

---

## Thank You {.center}

**Paper:** [DOI: 10.1016/j.awe.2025.100097](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.awe.2025.100097)

**Interactive version:** This website!

🐱 Ask the AI assistant for more details →
